goblet:correct: And so the Dread Pirate Roberts chose... wisely.
He had listened. He had paid attention. And it had been enough.
Victory. As unlikely as it sometimes seemed.
The battle of wits concluded in the only way a good story allows.
He had done it. Against Vizzini. Against the odds. He had done it.
Vizzini's grand intellect was finally brought low by simple deduction.
The pirate smiled, knowing the game was won before the cups were lifted.
A sharp mind had defeated a loud mouth. As is only right and proper.
It was a moment of pure triumph for the farm boy from Florin.
And with that single choice, the balance of power shifted entirely.
The arrogance of the Sicilian was his ultimate undoing.
Westley had seen through the bluster to the truth beneath.
It was, perhaps, the greatest upset in the history of wit-battling.
The poison found its rightful, boastful home.
And the hero survived to fight another day.
And so the Dread Pirate Roberts chose... wisely. Indeed.

goblet:poisoned: The wrong goblet. And yet — the story was not over.
Iocane, as it turned out, had been survived before.
A setback. A painful, poisonous setback. But not the end.
He had drunk deeply of the wrong cup. But resistance, it seems, can be built.
And yet. And yet, the Dread Pirate Roberts remained standing. Barely. But standing.
It appeared Vizzini had won. Appeared being the operative word.
The poison took hold, but a pirate's constitution is a stubborn thing.
A foolish mistake? Perhaps. But a fatal one? We shall see.
The world spun, the lights dimmed, but the hero did not fall. Not completely.
Vizzini laughed, but he did not know the man he was laughing at.
Sometimes, a hero must walk through the valley of shadow.
The choice was wrong, but the preparation was right.
It was a dark moment, darker than the deepest ocean trench.
He had failed the test of wits, but there were other tests yet to come.

game:won: And with a final, breathless insult, the Sicilian fell.
The pirate stood victorious, immune and alive.
Thus ends the greatest mind of a generation. Or so he claimed.
He swooned, the poison heavy in his blood, but the immunity held.
He passed out, yes, but a true pirate always wakes up.
Revived by his years of preparation, he watched his foe fall.
A battle of wits concluded, and the princess was safe.
And with a final, breathless insult, the Sicilian fell...
